steps.observed: File exists, 163 lines, Status: Accepted
Present: What implementation language should Vera use, with detailed requirements
2 options: Rust and TypeScript with Bun Runtime, each with 5 sub-assessments
Present: describes spike implementations measuring 4 key operations with environment details

steps.action: Verify Evidence section with concrete data
steps.expected: Concrete spike timings or ecosystem data
steps.observed: 5 benchmark tables: tree-sitter parsing (Rust 16.9ms vs Bun 29.7ms), file traversal (38.7ms vs 17.6ms), CLI cold start (0.51ms vs 5.09ms), binary sizes (2.6-3.1MB vs 32-80MB), ecosystem comparison (8-capability rating table)
Rust chosen with 5 rationale points
Gains (4), Trade-offs (4), Mitigations (3)
4 follow-up items
File exists, 143 lines, Status: Accepted
Present: What storage backend should Vera use, with 3 storage need categories
2 options: SQLite+sqlite-vec+Tantivy and LanceDB+Tantivy, each with detailed pros/cons
SQLite: 7596 chunks/sec (1316.6ms total) vs LanceDB: 241440 chunks/sec (41.4ms total) for 10K chunks
Vector: SQLite p50=9.731ms, p95=10.013ms vs LanceDB p50=1.945ms, p95=2.809ms. BM25: Tantivy p50=0.067ms
SQLite+sqlite-vec+Tantivy chosen with 6 rationale points
Gains (6), Trade-offs (4), Mitigations (3)
5 follow-up items
File exists, 134 lines, Status: Accepted
Present: Which embedding model should Vera use for semantic vector search
3 options: Qwen3-Embedding-8B (4096-dim), BAAI/bge-en-icl (4096-dim), Qwen3-Embedding-0.6B (1024-dim)

steps.action: Verify Evidence uses Vera own metrics (CRITICAL)
steps.expected: Recall and MRR from Vera eval harness, not external benchmarks
steps.observed: Vera-specific metrics confirmed: Recall@1=0.0952, Recall@5=0.4921, Recall@10=0.6627, MRR=0.2814. Per-category breakdowns across 5 workload categories.
Qwen3-Embedding-8B as primary, Qwen3-0.6B as lightweight fallback
Gains (4), Trade-offs (4), Mitigations (4)
5 follow-up items
File exists, 151 lines, Status: Accepted
Present: How should Vera split source code files into chunks for embedding and retrieval
3 options: Sliding-Window (50 lines/10 overlap), File-Level (entire file/split at 150), Symbol-Aware (AST-based via tree-sitter)
Symbol-Aware MRR=0.3792, Recall@5=0.5873 vs Sliding-Window MRR=0.2814, Recall@5=0.4921 vs File-Level MRR=0.2646
Output ratio vs sliding-window baseline: Symbol-Aware 0.86 (14% more compact), File-Level 0.80 (20% more compact). Quality-per-unit: Symbol-Aware 0.44, Sliding-Window 0.28, File-Level 0.33
Symbol-aware (AST-based) as primary, sliding-window as Tier 0 fallback
Gains (6), Trade-offs (4), Mitigations (5)
6 follow-up items
File exists, 194 lines, Status: Accepted
Implementation Language decided as Rust, references ADR-001
Storage Backend decided as SQLite+sqlite-vec+Tantivy, references ADR-002
Embedding Model decided as Qwen3-Embedding-8B, references ADR-003
Chunking Strategy decided as Symbol-Aware (tree-sitter AST), references ADR-004
Retrieval Pipeline Shape decided as Hybrid BM25+Vector+RRF+Reranking, references cross-ADR evidence

steps.action: Count open questions (must be at most 2)
steps.observed: Exactly 2 open questions: (1) Exact Retrieval Pipeline Parameters, (2) Graph-Lite Metadata Scope

summary: Tested 5 assertions: all 5 passed. All ADR files exist with the required 7-section format, contain concrete benchmark data from Vera own evaluation suite, compare 2+ options each, and the decision summary confirms all 5 major architecture questions are decided with exactly 2 open questions remaining.
